title: MFA Code Theft — Fake IT Helpdesk
intro: A message claims to be IT Support. Your goal: avoid sharing MFA codes, verify the sender, and report suspicious requests.
advice: Never share MFA/OTP codes with anyone.
Verify support requests by calling the official IT number (out-of-band).
Report suspicious messages to your security/IT team.

n1.text: Hi, this is IT Support. We detected unusual sign-in attempts on your account. Are you trying to log in right now?

choices.label: No. What’s the ticket number? I’ll call IT using the official number.
choices.explain: Good: you’re verifying through a trusted channel.

choices.label: Yes, I’m logging in. Can you help me quickly?
choices.explain: Risky: you’re giving the attacker momentum and confirming activity.

choices.label: Who are you? Prove it.
choices.explain: Decent: skepticism is good, but you still need out-of-band verification.

n2.text: We’re slammed. Faster fix: I’ll send a verification code. Read it back so I can secure your account.

choices.label: Send it.
choices.explain: Bad: support should not need your MFA code.

choices.label: No. I will contact IT directly. Do not message me again.
choices.explain: Correct: refuse the code request and verify via official channels.

n3.text: Okay. To confirm you’re the owner, what’s your email and the last 4 digits of your phone number?

choices.label: My email is ___ and last 4 is ____.
choices.explain: Unsafe: you shared personal identifiers with an unverified contact.

choices.label: I’m not sharing that. I’ll reach IT through the official helpdesk.
choices.explain: Good: refuse and use a trusted channel.

n4.text: You receive a 6-digit MFA code. If you read it out loud, an attacker can use it to log in as you.

choices.label: Read the code to them.
choices.explain: Compromised: this is how MFA bypass scams work.

choices.label: Do NOT share. Block and report the attempt.
choices.explain: Correct: never share MFA codes. Report it.

end_good.text: ✅ Safe outcome: You refused to share MFA codes and verified through official channels.

end_bad_soft.text: ⚠️ Outcome: You shared personal identifiers. That increases risk (account recovery scams, targeted phishing).

end_bad.text: ❌ Compromised: Sharing the MFA code allowed the attacker to log in.
